MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Kirkhill-Ta Co
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Genevieve Runnestrand, as successor-in-interest to, and as wrongful death heir of Wayne Runnestrand, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KIRKHILL-TA CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kirkhill-TA Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' first, second, third and fourth causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Runnestrand was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not support its motion with evidence that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability, precluding defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' causes of action. Mr. Pierce's declaration is insufficient to do so. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Dana Freed of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10602 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Gencorp Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Genevieve Runnestrand, as successor-in-interest to, and as wrongful death heir of Wayne Runnestrand, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GENCORP INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant GenCorp Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' first, second, third and fourth causes of action are denied. Plaintiffs concede that defendant sustained its initial burden or, at a minimum, they do not argue that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Runnestrand and the declaration of Dr. Cohen create a triable issue whether Mr. Runnestrand's exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant (v-44 and v-52 rubber) was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing lung cancer.
